title: NA-ULIP
word_text:
How Do ULIPs Help In Fund Generation?
Building a future for yourself and your loved ones is an activity that might be significantly dependent on planning your finances right. For example, if you have a specific goal to fulfil in the future, such as buying a house, you may want to start saving right away to help you achieve that goal. Savings alone may or may not be sufficient to help you turn your dreams into reality. Growing your money through investments, on the other hand, may help you keep up with the times and face inflation without compromising on your goals. A ULIP could be one such fund generation option for you to start your investment journey today.
When it comes to ULIPs, your premium is invested into various types of funds, which provide market linked returns. ULIPs could be considered long-term plans, which means that they may tend to offer significant returns in the long run, making them more suitable for people who seek fund generation through ULIPs for their long-term goals.
Before we dive into how you may use ULIPs for fund generation, and why you may find them suitable for yourself, let’s look at how ULIPs are structured, and how they work.
How do ULIPs work?
Unit-linked Insurance Plans, also known as ULIPs, are primarily life insurance products. However, these plans also offer the avenue of fund generation via the investment aspect. One may call it a dual benefit plan, where the policyholder can protect their family against the possibility of something unfortunate, as well as enable wealth building for their collective as well as individual future.
So, how do ULIPs work? You can start with an online ULIP calculator to get an estimate of the returns you may expect from these plans. This might also help you through the process of shortlisting and choosing the plans.
Once you know which ULIP you want to buy, you can complete the purchase process for the plan, online or offline. You are required to pay the premiums regularly as per selected frequencies to keep the policy in force.
As long as the policy is in force, you are offered a life cover. This may enable you to create financial security for your family, for them to face the circumstances that could arise after you are gone. The other aspect of the plan is fund generation, which may let you fulfil your long-term dreams.
Based on your goals, you should choose the right sort of ULIP funds. There are multiple funds available that may offer different levels of risks and returns. The choices may vary based on your risk appetite.
Why ULIPs may be the ideal choice for fund generation?
There may be several plans you could choose and put together to create your investment portfolio. Why should ULIPs be one of them, especially if you want to opt for fund generation? Let us look at some of the key reasons why ULIPs might work for fund generation.
Ease of Management
When you buy a ULIP, fund managers can manage the investment side of the plan on your behalf. Basis the high-level directions you give to your fund manager, like risk tolerance, investment horizon, financial goals, etc., the fund manager will manage your portfolio on your behalf.  This might let you breathe easy, without having to worry about which securities to invest in, when to buy or sell assets, when to switch between funds, etc. Alternately, you may even choose to manage the portfolio yourself, wherein the fund manager will only execute or help you in your requests.
Let’s You Create a Balance
You have a choice of funds when it comes to ULIPs. Your ULIP fund generation journey may depend on your risk appetite as well as your goals. You might want to choose the fund options accordingly. ULIPs can allow you to choose the funds that you feel are right for you. Depending on what you are looking for, you can make your portfolio lean towards debt or equity or create a balance of both.
Suitable for Long-Term Goals
If you choose ULIPs for fund generation, remember that these come with a lock-in period of five years. Furthermore, since they work through the power of compounding, they may offer considerable results in the long term rather than in the near future. Thus, you may find it suitable to stay invested in these plans for the long term. This may allow you to create investment discipline, which means that you may be more inclined towards maintaining your investments.
Tax Benefits
Even when used for fund generation, ULIPs like other life insurance plans, offer you tax benefits as well. The premium you pay is eligible for tax deduction under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961, as per the provisions stated therein. The death benefits you receive too are tax exempt under the Act. Remember that these benefits can only be availed under the old tax regime.
Types of ULIP Funds
There are a few types of ULIP funds available, which you can choose from. These are as follows:
Equity Funds
Equity funds are high-risk, high-reward funds. These funds primarily invest in listed equity stocks and are further classified as large-cap, mid-cap, small-cap funds, basis the kind of stock they invest in. Equity funds aim at capital appreciation through market-linked returns and are suitable for investors with a high-risk appetite.
Debt Funds
Under these funds, your premium is invested in the debt market, which includes debt-based securities like government securities, corporate bonds, etc. As these are fixed income instruments, debt funds carry a lower level of risk.
Liquid Funds
If you have a low risk appetite and are comfortable with relatively slow growth, you may like to choose liquid or cash funds. Here, investment is done in money-market instruments, short term debt securities that have a high credit rating. These can be redeemed easily, hence making them an ideal option for short term goals.
Hybrid/Balanced Funds
These funds rely on a mixed asset allocation in both equity instruments and debt securities. This gives you the benefit of capital appreciation from the equity investments and reliable earnings from the debt instruments. The risk level is moderate in these funds as it is balanced between equity and debt instruments.
Fund generation can be an important aspect of your overall financial planning, as your future goals may depend on it. With ULIPs, you might be able to benefit from a structured plan that offers you the opportunity to build your wealth while also ensuring a secure future for your loved ones.
Disclaimer
IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER. Investment in ULIPs is subject to risks associated with the capital markets. The policy holder is solely responsible for his/her decisions while investing in ULIPs. The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Ltd., Regd. office Address: Bajaj Allianz House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006, Reg. Tax benefits as per prevailing Income tax laws shall apply. Please check with your tax consultant for eligibility.
full_pdf_text:
How Do ULIPs Help In Fund Generation?
Building a future for yourself and your loved ones is an activity that might be significantly dependent on planning your finances right. For example, if you have a specific goal to fulfil in the future, such as buying a house, you may want to start saving right away to help you achieve that goal. Savings alone may or may not be sufficient to help you turn your dreams into reality. Growing your money through investments, on the other hand, may help you keep up with the times and face inflation without compromising much on your goals. A Unit Linked Insurance Plan (ULIP) could be one such fund generation option for you to start your investment journey today.
When it comes to ULIPs, your premium is invested into various types of market linked funds, which provide market linked returns. ULIPs could be considered long-term plans, which means that they may tend to offer significant returns in the long run, making them more suitable for people who seek fund generation through market linked instruments for their long-term goals.
Before we dive into how you may use ULIPs for fund generation, and why you may find them suitable for yourself, let’s look at how ULIPs are structured, and how they work?
How do ULIPs work?
Unit-linked Insurance Plans, also known as ULIPs, are primarily life insurance products. However, these plans also offer the avenue of fund generation via the investment aspect. One may call it a dual benefit plan, where the policyholder can protect their family against the possibility of something unfortunate, as well as enable wealth building for their collective as well as individual financial future.
So, how do ULIPs work? You can start with an online ULIP calculator to get an estimate of the returns you may expect from these plans. This might also help you through the process of shortlisting and choosing the plans.
Once you know which ULIP you want to buy, you can complete the purchase process for the plan, online or offline. You are required to pay the premiums regularly as per selected frequencies to keep the policy in force.
As long as the policy is in force, you are offered a life cover. This may enable you to create financial security for your family, for them to face the circumstances that could arise after you are gone. The other aspect of the plan is fund generation, which may let you fulfil your long-term dreams.
Based on your goals, you should choose the suitable ULIP funds. There are multiple funds available that may offer different levels of risks and returns. The choices may vary based on your risk appetite.
Why ULIPs may be one of the preferred choice for fund generation?
There may be several plans you could choose and put together to create your investment portfolio. Why should ULIPs be one of them, especially if you want to opt for fund generation? Let us look at some of the key reasons why ULIPs might work for fund generation.
 Ease of Management
When you buy a ULIP, fund managers can manage the investment side of the plan on your behalf. Basis the directions you give to your fund manager, like risk tolerance, investment horizon, financial goals, etc., the fund manager will manage your portfolio on your behalf. This might let you breathe easy, about which securities to invest in, when to buy or sell assets, when to switch between funds, etc.
 Let’s You Create a Balance
You have a choice of funds when it comes to ULIPs. Your ULIP fund generation journey may depend on your risk appetite as well as your goals. You might want to choose the fund options accordingly. ULIPs can allow you to choose the funds that you feel are suitable for you as per your life goals. Depending on what you are looking for, you can make your portfolio lean towards debt or equity or create a balance of both.
 Suitable for Long-Term Goals
If you choose ULIPs for fund generation, remember that these come with a lock-in period of five years. Furthermore, since they work through the power of compounding, they may offer considerable results in the long term rather than in the near future. Thus, you may find it suitable to stay invested in these plans for the long term. This may allow you to create investment discipline, which means that you may be more inclined towards maintaining your investments.
 Tax Benefits
Even when used for fund generation, ULIPs like other life insurance plans, offer you tax benefits as well. The premium you pay is eligible for tax deduction under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961, as per the provisions stated therein. As per the amendments under Section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961, the maturity benefit received from ULIPs bought after 1st February,2021 with annual premiums less than Rs.2.5 lacs, are tax exempt. However, for ULIPs with aggregate annual premium exceeding Rs. 2.5lacs, bought after 1st Februrary,2021, the gains will be taxable as capital gains, depending upon the type of funds you invest in. For all the ULIPs bought before 1st February,2021, irrespective of the premium amounts, the returns are completely tax-free. The death benefits you receive too are tax exempt under the Act. Remember that these benefits can only be availed under the old tax regime.
Types of ULIP Funds
There are a few types of ULIP funds available, which you can choose from. However, it may be noted that past performance of funds are not indicative of future performance. These are as follows:
1. Equity Funds
Equity funds are high-risk, high-reward funds. These funds primarily invest in listed equity stocks and are further classified as large-cap, mid-cap, small-cap funds, basis the kind of stock they invest in.
Equity funds aim at capital appreciation through market-linked returns and are suitable for investors with a high-risk appetite.
2. Debt Funds
Under these funds, your premium is invested in the debt market, which includes debt-based securities like government securities, corporate bonds, etc. As these are fixed income instruments, debt funds carry a lower level of risk. 3. Liquid Funds
If you have a low risk appetite and are comfortable with relatively slow growth, you may like to choose liquid or cash funds. Here, investment is done in money-market instruments, short term debt securities that have a high credit rating. These can be redeemed easily, hence making them an suitable option for short term goals. 4. Hybrid/Balanced Funds
These funds rely on a mixed asset allocation in both equity instruments and debt securities. This gives you the benefit of capital appreciation from the equity investments and reliable earnings from the debt instruments. The risk level is moderate in these funds as it is balanced between equity and debt instruments.
Fund generation can be an important aspect of your overall financial planning, as your future goals may depend on it. With ULIPs, you might be able to benefit from a structured plan that offers you the opportunity to build market linked wealth while also ensuring a secure financial future for your loved ones.
Disclaimer
IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER. Investment in ULIPs is subject to risks associated with the capital markets. The policy holder is solely responsible for his/her decisions while investing in ULIPs. The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. Tax benefits as per prevailing Income tax laws shall apply. Please check with your tax consultant for eligibility.
